steps.title: Expand and establish constraints
steps.content:
Expand $(3x^2 + a)(2x^2 + b)$: $$6x^4 + 3bx^2 + 2ax^2 + ab = 6x^4 + (3b+2a)x^2 + ab$$
Match this with $6x^4 + 17x^2 + 7$ to get two conditions:
  • Coefficient of $x^2$: $3b + 2a = 17$
  • Constant term: $ab = 7$

steps.title: Find all solutions via quadratic formula
steps.content:
From $ab = 7$, we have $b = \frac{7}{a}$. Substitute into $3b + 2a = 17$: $$\frac{21}{a} + 2a = 17$$
Multiply by $a$ and rearrange: $$2a^2 - 17a + 21 = 0$$
By the quadratic formula: $$a = \frac{17 \pm \sqrt{289 - 168}}{4} = \frac{17 \pm \sqrt{121}}{4} = \frac{17 \pm 11}{4}$$
So $a = \frac{28}{4} = 7$ (the integer solution) or $a = \frac{6}{4} = \frac{3}{2}$ (the non-integer solution).
Therefore, $a = 7$ and $c = \frac{3}{2}$.
